commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human maintainers need to choose the canonical Teams/CVI path and require conflict, CI, and security/owner review before merge.; Needs attention: The diff introduces security-sensitive media, DLP/audit, SSRF, recording-gate, and local-media display paths, and current security-fast still fails on production undici advisories. (sha=e072c0efb178957f1e1e552be5c3445195bba1d3)
commands.review_summary:
this PR adds Microsoft Teams CVI voice/video calls, Teams chat actions, governance features, realtime voice/TTS SDK additions, docs, and tests across the msteams, voice-call, OpenAI, and ElevenLabs surfaces.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new Teams voice/video/chat feature PR rather than a current-main bug reproduction; the contributor supplied live proof, but no failing current-main bug path is being reproduced.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainers need to choose the canonical Teams/CVI path and require conflict, CI, and security/owner review before merge.

target.title: feat(msteams): Microsoft Teams voice (CVI) + video + chat + governance
target.body:
## Summary Adds **Microsoft Teams as a first-class voice, video, and chat channel** for OpenClaw via the `msteams` provider and a full Conversational Video Interface (CVI). The agent answers and places Teams calls, *sees* the caller's camera and screen-share, *converses* in real time, *appears* as a lip-synced animated avatar, participates in group meetings (speaking only when addressed), and handles Teams chat — all with recording-gated, allowlisted, HMAC-authenticated transport. Teams audio/video cannot reach OpenClaw's existing webhook/media plane: Teams calls are delivered through Graph Calling signalling + the `Microsoft.Skype.Bots.Media` socket, which only run on .NET/Windows. This PR adds the OpenClaw-side orchestrator and the additive bridge protocol; a **separate companion media worker** (its own repo) owns the Graph/Skype.Bots.Media socket and renders the avatar tile. The three CVI pillars map directly onto what lands here: | Pillar | Meaning | In this PR | | --- | --- | --- | | **Perception** | the agent *sees* | inbound `video.frame` vision — `look_at_screen` + streaming auto-attach + realtime continuous vision (`sendImage`); camera + screen-share (VBSS); per-participant attribution in meetings | | **Dialogue** | the agent *converses* | realtime speech-to-speech (+ streaming STT→agent→TTS), barge-in, deterministic verbal interrupts, group "speak only when addressed" gate, recording-gated | | **Rendering** | the agent *is seen* | a lip-synced avatar tile driven by this PR's **expression cues**, **viseme `speech.marks`**, and **`show_to_caller`/`display.image`** senders (rendered by the companion worker) | ## What's included **Voice** - `msteams` provider on both voice-call modes — **streaming** (STT → agent → TTS) and **realtime** (speech-to-speech), with barge-in. - Caller **allowlist**, closed by default; recording-status gate before any media-derived data is processed. - **Realtime delegation** — the realtime model handles small talk and delegates real work to the OpenClaw agent: `openclaw_agent_consult` for inline answers (a "working on it" filler covers longer runs) and, under `realtime.toolPolicy: "owner"`, `openclaw_agent_task` for background jobs. - **Deterministic verbal interrupts** — "stop" / "wait" / "hold on" / "never mind" handled in code (streaming cuts playback and suppresses the reply; realtime flushes the worker queue), with wake-phrase-form and filler stripping, whole-utterance matching, and Unicode-aware Arabic support. - **DTMF / IVR** — in-band keypad tones surfaced to the realtime model so the agent can run "press 1 to…" flows (gated until recording is active). - **Bilingual Arabic/English** (opt-in) — detect, mirror, switch-with-caller, translate on request; the deterministic layers (interrupts, visemes) understand Arabic regardless. - **Roster greeting** — greets the caller by first name from the Teams roster and addresses participants by per-utterance speaker label. **Inbound video vision** - `video.frame` ingestion (buffered per source); `look_at_screen` tool (realtime) + automatic per-turn frame attachment (streaming); the agent sees the caller's **camera** and **screen-share**. - **Realtime continuous vision** — `RealtimeVoiceBridge.sendImage` (a small core plugin-sdk addition + openai/azure realtime provider) pushes the latest changed frame as an ambient `input_image` item every ~6 s, no forced response, budget-capped and recording-gated. - **Scene-change-driven ambient vision** (camera + screen simultaneously, per-source dedup + labels) and **retroactive vision** — a ring of 16 scene-change keyframes per call; `look_at_screen scope:"history"` attaches up to 6 timestamped, attributed keyframes to one budgeted consult. - Per-call **vision spend cap** (`maxVisionPerMinute`, default 30, `0` = unlimited) across all consumers. **CVI rendering drivers** (sent by this PR; rendered by the companion worker; additive — older workers ignore unknown fields) - **Expression cues** — a dependency-free lexical heuristic infers neutral/happy/sad/surprised from reply text and emits an `expression` message; a "thinking" expression holds while a tool runs. - **Viseme lip-sync** — `speech.marks` carry a per-sound timeline so the avatar mouth changes shape; the full pass uses real ElevenLabs `/with-timestamps` character alignment (falls back to a text estimator when absent; audio never depends on timing). - **Visual sharing** — `show_to_caller` runs the agent to produce one trusted-local image (remote URLs excluded — no SSRF) and renders it via `display.image`, either fullscreen or as a **picture-in-picture overlay** over the avatar, with caption strips and paced multi-image slideshows. **Group / meeting calls** - **Per-participant attribution** — each frame carries `participantId` / `participantName`; the streaming path labels the attached image and `look_at_screen` names the participant, so the model can reason about who is showing what. Active-speaker camera follow / re-select. - **Speak only when addressed** — in a group call (2+ humans) the assistant stays silent until addressed by name (`wakePhrases`), with a short follow-up window; 1:1 always responds. Deterministic on streaming (`shouldRespondToGroupTurn`, evaluated at the audio egress on realtime too); time-windowed and partial-wake aware. **Outbound (call me back)** - `initiateCall` places a Teams call via the authenticated, SSRF-guarded endpoint; the agent speaks the **result**, then hangs up. Delivery via Teams **chat message** (default) or **voice call-back**. - **Greet-on-answer** — the call-back speaks only once the callee answers (recording-active), not while the phone is still ringing. - Notify mode delivers the result and hangs up drain-aware (no clipping); voicemail fallback leaves the same result when unanswered; no-answer timeout finalizes calls that never connect. **Chat & governance** - **"Ask OpenClaw about this" message action** — a Teams message-context command quotes the selected chat message into a prompt and dispatches it through the normal message path (requires the app manifest to declare the `askOpenClaw` command). - **Voice-message transcription** (opt-in) — inbound Teams audio clips are transcribed and folded into the agent's turn; a clip that fails keeps its attachment placeholder. - **Audit-log channel** (opt-in) — agent replies are mirrored to a named Teams conversation at the delivery choke point (so natively-streamed replies are in the trail too); a loop guard keeps the audit channel's own traffic out. - **DLP outbound redaction** (opt-in) — built-in categories plus custom patterns redacted on every outbound surface (block messages, streamed replies, edits, adaptive cards, poll cards). Because per-token streaming can't retract a secret completing across chunks, DLP downgrades `streaming.mode: "partial"` to `"progress"`. **Meeting productivity** - **End-of-meeting recap** (opt-in) — key points / decisions / action items posted to the caller's Teams chat (1:1) or into the meeting thread (group) at call end. - **On-demand minutes** mid-call (`post_meeting_minutes`), a real **OOXML `.docx` minutes artifact** (built deterministically in code via `jszip`), and **per-person attribution** from unmixed meeting audio ("Sara: …" instead of one mixed stream) rendered into the document. **Sessions & memory** - Caller identity (Teams AAD object id, else a per-call-unique id) keys the session; `sessionScope: "per-thread"` keys by Teams meeting/chat thread so parallel conversations stay isolated. PSTN falls through to per-phone. ## Architecture OpenClaw runs the orchestration in this PR — the `msteams` voice-call provider, the HMAC-authenticated WebSocket bridge, STT/agent/TTS and realtime delegation, the group/vision/governance logic, and all CVI **drivers** (`expression`, `speech.marks`, `display.image`, `sendImage`). A **separate companion Windows/.NET media worker** (its own repo) owns the Graph Calling signalling and the `Microsoft.Skype.Bots.Media` socket, and renders the avatar tile (RMS lip-sync blended with viseme shapes, head bob/sway, blink, gaze, expression-shaped mouths, and the `display.image` overlay). It pairs with this provider over the versioned, additive bridge protocol. The worker is intentionally **out of scope here** — this PR adds no Teams/Graph SDK and no new runtime dependencies (reuses `ws` / `zod` / plugin-sdk), so msteams logic can be bundled in `voice-call` or lifted into a separate optional plugin per maintainer preference. ## Recent reliability fixes - **Echo self-answer fix** — `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` now defaults **on** (default-off was a live self-answer bug); the echo guard runs on a playout clock, and the opening greeting allows no barge-in until the caller's first real turn, so a speakerphone echo can't make the bot re-greet in a loop. - **Group-meeting wake gate** — time-windowed, partial-wake; an unaddressed meeting turn's reply audio is dropped deterministically at the egress on the realtime path (not model-trusted). - **Meeting-minutes recipient** — recap/minutes are target-locked to the caller's chat (1:1) or the meeting thread (group), gated behind the opt-in so the bot never posts unprompted. - **Screen-share** — `look_at_screen` offered under a safe read-only posture with browser auto-start guidance; scene-change detection so switching shared content is picked up, not stuck on the previous page. - **Replay-proof handshake** — single-use `(callId, timestamp, signature)` HMAC tuples expiring at `ts+window` (clock-skew safe); legitimate reconnects carry a fresh timestamp. - **Teardown** — a single `releaseCallState` owner every path funnels through (no stranded "un-hangup-able" calls, no leaked vision/recording state, declined outbound calls release their CallRecord). ## Behavior changes Defaults preserve prior behavior except two flagged changes: - `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` flipped **off → on** (fixes the self-answer echo bug; set `false` to restore, tune via `echoSuppressionWindowMs` / `echoBargeInRms`). - Outbound call-backs honor `outbound.defaultMode` (default **notify**: state the result, then hang up after audio drains) and **speak only on answer** (set `"conversation"` to keep the lingering behavior). All other additions are opt-in: `msteams.meetingRecap`, `msteams.bilingual`, `channels.msteams.transcribeVoiceMessages`, `channels.msteams.auditChannel`, `channels.msteams.dlp.enabled`. ## Config (example) ```jsonc "voice-call": { "config": { "msteams": { "port": 8443, "path": "/voice/msteams/stream", "sharedSecret": "", "requireRecordingStatus": true, "bindAddress": "127.0.0.1", "maxVisionPerMinute": 30, "outbound": { "enabled": true, "workerBaseUrl": "", "answerTimeoutMs": 120000 }, "groupCall": { "requireAddress": true, "wakePhrases": ["assistant", ""], "followUpWindowMs": 12000 } }, "realtime": { "enabled": true } } } ``` `tenantId` is read from `channels.msteams.tenantId`. `groupCall` only affects calls with 2+ humans; 1:1 always responds. `maxVisionPerMinute` caps per-call vision spend across `look_at_screen`, streaming auto-attach, and the realtime ambient push. Audio is **PCM 16 kHz**. ### Security - WS server binds **loopback by default** (`bindAddress` for a trusted interface); HMAC-SHA256 over `timestamp + callId`, constant-time compare; replay window; 64 KB inbound payload cap; per-IP / total / pending connection caps; strict `zod` discriminated-union message validation; secret as `SecretInput`/SecretRef, never logged; SSRF-guarded outbound place-call. Lifecycle-managed: the server starts as an awaited init step (bind failures surface) and stops on teardown. ## Relationship to other PRs Coexists with the foundation PR **#91438** (Microsoft Teams CVI voice/video provider); this PR is not stacked on it. ## Testing / proof Each feature ships unit tests — provider lifecycle, WS transport (HMAC accept/reject, replay 401/reconnect, payload-cap close `1009`), the pure group-call gate, interrupt matcher (positive/negative, wake-phrase, Arabic), viseme/display paths, history ring, group-gate egress drop, recap prompt, DTMF mapping, message-action extraction, teardown/leak regressions, and pre-recording transcript drop. Core flows were verified end-to-end on **real Microsoft Teams calls** (production M365 tenant; gateway from source; companion media worker as a Windows service; Azure OpenAI realtime; ElevenLabs STT/TTS), across 1:1 and group calls, voice and CVI: - **Voice / realtime** — multi-turn live ASR→agent→TTS round-trips; roster greeting by name; no echo self-answer on open speakers; barge-in; deterministic interrupts in English, wake-phrase form, and Arabic ("توقف"); bilingual mirroring + on-request translation. - **Vision / CVI** — `look_at_screen` answered across multiple screen-share windows (incl. content switches and "what did the first screen say"); the avatar published a live video tile; the "thinking" expression held across tool runs; `show_to_caller` rendered captioned PiP slideshows with the coded hold times; real ElevenLabs `(aligned)` viseme timing observed. - **Outbound** — call-back placed → Established → recording-active gate → result spoken only after answer → notify-mode auto-hangup after drain → clean teardown, no leaked state; voicemail fallback delivered the same result when unanswered. - **Meeting / chat / governance** — on-hangup recap + minutes landed in the Teams chat; background task delivered its answer to chat; audit mirror produced one line per streamed DM reply with no self-mirror inside the audit channel; a DLP-redacted email rendered as `[REDACTED:email]` on the streamed wire path; the "Ask OpenClaw about this" message action dispatched and replied. Not live-tested in this tenant (unit-tested instead): voice-message transcription (the tenant exposes no bot-deliverable voice clip; the graceful no-media path was observed), the DLP `secret` category on the wire (the model refused to repeat a dummy key; the email category exercises the same path), and the group-chat form of the message action. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Microsoft Teams voice/video/CVI + chat as a first-class OpenClaw channel — the `msteams` voice provider answers/places real Teams calls, sees the caller's screen-share/camera, renders a lip-synced avatar, and runs chat governance (DLP / audit / minutes). - **Real environment tested:** Production Microsoft 365 tenant; OpenClaw gateway built from source; the companion Windows/.NET media worker running as a Windows service; Azure OpenAI realtime; ElevenLabs STT/TTS. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Enabled `voice-call` `provider: "msteams"` + `realtime` and the `channels.msteams` governance blocks in `openclaw.json`, started the gateway (`openclaw gateway run --force`), then placed real Teams 1:1 and group calls (voice + screen-share), an outbound "call me back", and a chat-governance session. - **Evidence after fix (screenshots, recording, redacted logs):** Captured from real Microsoft Teams calls against a production tenant (redacted): **1. Be-seen avatar — the bot publishes its avatar as a live video tile** **2. `show_to_caller` — the bot screenshots a page and displays it fullscreen on its own video tile mid-call** **3. Outbound "call me back" — the bot rings the caller on Teams** **4. Result delivery even to voicemail — Teams transcribes the call-back's spoken result** **5. Screen recording of a live call** https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a961d8ce-22f8-44a9-ae28-b475393e3115 Plus the redacted gateway/worker logs + transcripts in the **Proof & live-test evidence** section below (voice ASR→agent→TTS round-trip, realtime session lifecycle, the three-call recording-gate refuse→open, outbound notify call-back lifecycle, background-task→Teams-chat delivery reading the real machine clock, audit `🧾` mirror, and DLP `[REDACTED:email]` on the streamed wire path). - **Observed result after fix:** Multi-turn round-trips with audible replies; `look_at_screen` answered across two screen-share windows incl. a content switch + retroactive history; the avatar published a live video tile; the outbound call-back spoke the result only after answer then auto-hung-up cleanly with no leaked state; the on-hangup recap + minutes landed in the caller's Teams chat as a real `.docx`; DLP redacted a secret on the live streamed reply. - **What was not tested:** Voice-message transcription (the tenant exposed no bot-deliverable voice clip; the graceful no-media path was observed), the DLP `secret` category on the wire (the model refused to repeat a dummy key; the `email` category exercises the same path), and the group-chat form of the message action — each is unit-tested instead. - **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Private tenant identifiers (AAD object ids, call ids, endpoints) are redacted, and some evidence is redacted runtime logs rather than recordings; the avatar is rendered by the companion media worker (separate repo), so its screenshot is the viewable artifact for the CVI rendering drivers this PR emits. - **Before evidence (optional but encouraged):** The pre-fix `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` default-off self-answer bug was reproduced before the fix (a speakerphone echo made the bot re-greet itself in a loop); fixed by defaulting it on plus the playout-clock echo guard. ## Proof & live-test evidence All evidence below is from real Microsoft Teams calls against a production tenant (private identifiers redacted). ![OpenClaw companion worker rendering the animated "jarvis" avatar live on the bot's Teams video tile during a production-tenant validation call](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/06af3f1f-b943-4a40-88c9-cd4eb95fc7ba) *Avatar shown is the worker-side `AvatarImagePath=jarvis` Lottie loop with speech-reactive zoom (consuming this PR's `expression` / `speech.marks` viseme / `display.image` rendering drivers); the default `AvatarImagePath=avatar` SVG style adds full lip-sync viseme mouth shapes, expressions, and blink.* ### Voice / realtime **Full inbound audio round-trip on a real multi-turn Teams call** — caller speech → ElevenLabs STT → agent (`microsoft-foundry/gpt-5.4`) → ElevenLabs TTS, across multiple turns (~70 s). Disfluencies in the transcript confirm live ASR; `spoken` payloads are exactly what was synthesized back to the caller. ``` user (STT): "How are you, man?" asst (spoken): "I'm doing well, man - thanks. What can I help you with?" user (STT): "Tell me more about you." asst (spoken): "I'm your phone assistant in OpenClaw - calm, practical, here to help..." ```
Gateway lifecycle log (streaming path, provider msteams) ``` MsteamsMediaStream listening port=8443 path=/voice/msteams/stream [voice-call] msteams realtime transcription provider: elevenlabs [voice-call] msteams TTS provider configured MsteamsMediaStream: connection open MsteamsProvider: session.start callId= threadId= caller.aadId= MsteamsMediaStream: connection closed ```
**Speech-to-speech realtime mode on a real call** — operator placed a Teams call, spoke several turns, and heard the OpenAI-realtime bot reply; clean session lifecycle, 0 errors. **Verbal interrupts / barge-in on the ~7.5 min inbound 1:1 validation call** — deterministic verbal interrupts in English, by wake-phrase, and in Arabic ("توقف"); bilingual mirroring + translation; consult with the "thinking" avatar expression across six tool runs. ### Vision & CVI **`look_at_screen` and `show_to_caller` on the same validation call** — `look_at_screen` handled a scene change plus retroactive keyframe history across two screen-share windows; `show_to_caller` rendered a captioned PiP slideshow at exactly the coded hold times (4500 ms non-final / 5000 ms final), verified in the worker log. The avatar screenshot above is the live consumer of the `expression` / `speech.marks` viseme / `display.image` drivers this PR emits. ### Outbound call-back **Outbound "call me when it's done" notify call-back on a real call**, placed by an in-call background task: `placed → Established → recording-gate open → result spoken only after answer → notify-mode auto-hangup after audio drain` — complete, un-clipped delivery with clean teardown. **`openclaw_agent_task` background task → Teams chat delivery on a real call.** Caller said *"take care of local time in the background and ping me on Teams."* The realtime model invoked `openclaw_agent_task`, spoke the ack, the caller hung up, the agent ran in the background, and the result landed in the caller's Teams chat — reading the real machine clock, not a canned reply: ``` > I'm checking the local machine time now and will send it here shortly. > Local machine time: 2026-06-04 05:28:13 +00:00. ``` ### Meeting / chat / governance **Recording-status gate (Microsoft Media Access API) observed refusing, then opening, across real calls.** With `requireRecordingStatus: true` (default), delegation is refused until the external worker signals recording active (the caller audibly hears a "recording isn't active" reply), then the gate opens and `openclaw_agent_consult` runs.
Redacted gateway log across real calls (recording gate) ``` # Call A — no recording signal → consult refused MsteamsProvider: realtime session.start callId=0c005180… caller.aadId= MsteamsMediaStream: connection closed 0c005180… # Call B — worker reports recording active → gate opens → consult runs MsteamsProvider: realtime session.start callId=2d005480… caller.aadId= MsteamsProvider: recording.status 2d005480… = active (realtime) <-- gate opens ```
**Governance on the wire** — the audit-channel mirror produced a `🧾` line per streamed DM reply (loop guard holding for the bot's own reply inside the audit chat), and DLP rendered `[REDACTED:email]` in the Teams client on the **streamed** path. On-hangup recap plus on-demand minutes both landed in chat. ### Message action — "Ask OpenClaw about this" The Teams message-context command quotes the selected chat message into a prompt and dispatches it through the normal message path; on the 2026-06-12 governance session it **dispatched and replied** in the Teams client. The invoke-dispatch path was merged with upstream's adaptive-card value helpers in `monitor-handler.ts` (both kept), with message-action extraction unit-covered. (The group-chat form is unit-tested, not live-tested in this tenant.) ### Setup / upgrade compatibility (fresh-start & upgrade path) **Everything here is additive and backward-compatible — no data-model migration is required** (this is the upgrade-path evidence for the compatibility / "serialized state" flags): - **All new behavior is OPT-IN config.** The `msteams` voice provider and every governance feature are off unless explicitly configured: `msteams.meetingRecap`, `msteams.bilingual`, `channels.msteams.transcribeVoiceMessages`, `channels.msteams.auditChannel`, `channels.msteams.dlp.enabled`, plus the `msteams.groupCall` / `msteams.outbound` blocks. A config that doesn't set these behaves exactly as before. - **Existing voice-call providers are unaffected.** `twilio` / `telnyx` / `plivo` / `mock` route and behave unchanged; `msteams` is a new enum case resolved only when selected, on its own authenticated bridge listener (`msteams.port`/`path`), not the public webhook plane. - **plugin-sdk additions are optional with a no-op facade.** `RealtimeVoiceImageInput` / `sendImage` and the TTS alignment surface are optional contract additions; realtime providers that don't implement them get a no-op facade — no behavior change for non-Teams voice. - **No persisted-state migration.** The files flagged as "serialized state" are **test fixtures** (`elevenlabs/*.test.ts`, `openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`) and **in-memory per-call state** (`msteams-realtime.ts`, `session-runtime.ts`); nothing persisted crosses versions. `bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts` is **regenerated tooling output** (the validation schema for the new opt-in keys), not user data — old configs validate as before. - **Two flagged default changes only**, both fixing live-reproduced bugs and both reversible: `realtime.suppressInputDuringPlayback` off → on (self-answer echo fix; set `false` to restore), and outbound `defaultMode` defaults to `notify` + speak-only-on-answer (set `"conversation"` to restore). **New real `.docx` minutes.** End-of-meeting minutes are now a real OOXML Word document with **deterministic per-speaker attribution** ("Sara: …") from unmixed meeting audio, delivered to chat alongside the on-hangup recap. Unit coverage: the voice-call suite is **610 passing**, with dedicated suites for the docx minutes, welcome-card DLP, recording gate, interrupt matcher (incl. Arabic), and DLP cards. ## Acknowledgements Contributed by the team at the **Ports, Customs and Free Zone Corporation (PCFC)** — Dubai, United Arab Emirates — bringing Microsoft Teams voice & video (the Conversational Video Interface) to OpenClaw for public-sector use. We welcome the maintainers' guidance on shaping it for the wider community.
target.author: ahenawy

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
